title: phlox stansburyi var. viscida
intro: Phlox stansburyi var. viscida, also known as sticky phlox, is a perennial plant in the phlox family that is native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of sticky phlox include treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
